title: ICBC AgentsГўВҖВҷ Deposits (BC)
body_text:
ICBC AgentsГўВҖВҷ Deposits (BC)
1	Introduction to ICBC AgentsГўВҖВҷ Deposits
1.1	Overview of ICBC AgentsГўВҖВҷ Deposits
An insurance agent or broker accepting payments for the Insurance Corporation of British Columbia (ICBC) can request to open an account with a local financial institution for the purpose of depositing ICBC payments. The agent opens the account on behalf of ICBC with ICBCГўВҖВҷs approval.
ICBC agents prepare deposits using ICBC Deposit Slip, Form 1126. On the day deposits are made, your financial institution credits the ICBC agentГўВҖВҷs account, submits an ICBC Deposit Slip on ServiceNow, and processes cheques included in the deposit in Branch Capture. Central 1 generates a pre-authorized debit (PAD) against each account (the sweep), consolidates the ICBC deposits from all financial institutions, and forwards the credit to ICBCГўВҖВҷs Royal Bank account.
Since the accounts are credit accounts for ICBC agent deposits only, they must never be in an overdraft position and canГўВҖВҷt have transaction fees or overdraft fees posted to them. However, financial institutions can obtain reimbursement for regular account service charges from ICBC.

Deposit Processing Part
Online Return System (ORS) Part

Business Accounts Part

manuals@central1.com
or speak with your Central 1 Relationship Manager

Contact Central 1 in the following circumstances:
There is a problem with a PAD debited to an ICBC agentГўВҖВҷs account. These PADs must not be dishonoured.
An error in an ICBC agentГўВҖВҷs deposit is identified after the deposit is transmitted to Central 1 via Branch Capture.
To confirm ICBC employees authorized to inquire about ICBC accounts.
